info.name: Microsoft Azure PolicyClient
info.description: To use in policy authoring you can create a variable at a scope. Variables created at a scope can be shared between multiple policy definitions.

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the variable to operate on.

request.description: This operation deletes a variable, given its name and the subscription it was created in. The scope of a variable is the part of its ID preceding '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/variables/{variableName}'.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: No Content - the variable doesn't exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Creates Or Updates A Variable

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the variable to operate on.

request.description: This operation creates or updates a variable with the given subscription and name. Policy variables can only be used by a policy definition at the scope they are created or below.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the updated variable.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Created - Returns information about the new variable.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.
(Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.
(Required) The name of the variable to operate on.

request.description: This operation retrieves a single variable, given its name and the subscription it was created at.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the variable.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Retrieves All Variables That Are At This Subscription Level

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the target subscription.

request.description: This operation retrieves the list of all variables associated with the given subscription.

response.name: OK - Returns an array of variables.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.
(Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the management group.
(Required) The name of the variable to operate on.

request.description: This operation deletes a variable, given its name and the management group it was created in. The scope of a variable is the part of its ID preceding '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/variables/{variableName}'.

response.name: OK

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: No Content - the variable doesn't exist.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Creates Or Updates A Variable

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the management group.
(Required) The name of the variable to operate on.

request.description: This operation creates or updates a variable with the given  management group and name. Policy variables can only be used by a policy definition at the scope they are created or below.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the updated variable.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Created - Returns information about the new variable.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

(Required) The name of the variable to operate on.

request.description: This operation retrieves a single variable, given its name and the  management group it was created at.

response.name: OK - Returns information about the variable.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Retrieves All Variables That Are At This Management Group Level

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

variable.description: (Required) The ID of the management group.

request.description: This operation retrieves the list of all variables applicable to the management group.

response.name: OK - Returns an array of variables.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for the operation.
